description: MCP Integration - Add Model Context Protocol as a first-class mcp: step type, enabling native calls to 16,000+ MCP servers (GitHub, Slack, databases, cloud providers) without shell glue code

userStories.title: Add rmcp dependency and mcp feature flag
userStories.description: As a developer, I want rmcp and tokio as optional dependencies behind an mcp feature flag so the base binary stays small and MCP support is opt-in.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `rmcp = { version = "0.16", features = ["client", "transport-io"], optional = true }` to Cargo.toml
Add `tokio = { version = "1", features = ["rt", "macros"], optional = true }` to Cargo.toml
Add `serde_json` to dependencies if not already present (needed for MCP args)
Add `mcp = ["dep:rmcp", "dep:tokio"]` to [features] section
Do NOT add mcp to default features
`cargo build` succeeds without mcp feature (no regression)
`cargo build --features mcp` succeeds with mcp feature
`cargo test` passes (no regression)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add MCP data model structs to models.rs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want McpStepConfig and McpServerRef structs so MCP steps can be represented in the workflow data model.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `McpStepConfig` struct with fields: `server: McpServerRef`, `tool: String`, `args: Option<serde_json::Value>`
Add `McpServerRef` enum with variants: `Alias(String)` and `Inline { command: String, env: Option<HashMap<String, String>>, secrets: Option<Vec<String>> }`
Both types derive `Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize`
McpServerRef uses `#[serde(untagged)]` for transparent deserialization (string = Alias, object = Inline)
Add `mcp: Option<McpStepConfig>` field to the existing `Step` struct, gated with `#[cfg(feature = "mcp")]`
Unit test: deserialize McpStepConfig with alias server from JSON
Unit test: deserialize McpStepConfig with inline server from JSON
`cargo build` succeeds without mcp feature (Step struct unchanged)
`cargo build --features mcp` succeeds with new field
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: McpServerRef::Alias is a plain string (server alias from config.toml). McpServerRef::Inline has command, env, secrets fields for inline server definitions in YAML.

userStories.title: Add MCP server config parsing to config.rs
userStories.description: As a user, I want to define MCP server aliases in config.toml so workflows can reference servers by short name.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `McpServerConfig` struct with fields: `command: String`, `env: Option<HashMap<String, String>>`, `secrets: Option<Vec<String>>`, `timeout: Option<u64>`
McpServerConfig derives `Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, Default`
Add `mcp_servers: HashMap<String, McpServerConfig>` field to the `Config` struct (or equivalent), deserialized from `[mcp.servers.*]` TOML sections
Field defaults to empty HashMap when absent from config.toml
Unit test: parse config.toml with `[mcp.servers.github]` section containing command and secrets
Unit test: parse config.toml with multiple server definitions
Unit test: parse config.toml with no `[mcp]` section (empty HashMap, no error)
`cargo build` succeeds
`cargo test` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Config struct is in src/core/config.rs. The [mcp.servers] section is a TOML table of tables. McpServerConfig is NOT feature-gated — config parsing works regardless of mcp feature so users see helpful errors if they configure MCP servers without the feature enabled.

userStories.title: Parse and validate mcp: steps in YAML workflows
userStories.description: As a user, I want to write `mcp:` steps in my workflow YAML files so I can call MCP tools natively.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `mcp` field to `RawStep` struct in parser.rs (gated with `#[cfg(feature = "mcp")]`)
Deserialization handles both alias form (`mcp: { server: github, tool: create_issue, args: {...} }`) and inline form (`mcp: { server: { command: "npx ...", env: {...} }, tool: ..., args: {...} }`)
Validation enforces mutual exclusion: exactly one of `cmd`, `call`, or `mcp` must be set — error if zero or multiple
When mcp feature is disabled and YAML contains an `mcp:` field, emit a clear error: 'MCP steps require the mcp feature. Rebuild with: cargo build --features mcp'
Unit test: parse valid mcp step with alias server
Unit test: parse valid mcp step with inline server
Unit test: parse step with both cmd and mcp — error
Unit test: parse step with mcp but no tool — error
`cargo test --features mcp` passes
`cargo test` passes (no regression)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Parser is in src/core/parser.rs. RawStep is the intermediate deserialization struct that gets normalized into Step. The cmd/call/mcp mutual exclusion check should be in the normalize_step() or validate() function.

userStories.title: Create core MCP client module
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a core MCP client module that handles spawning servers, initializing connections, listing tools, and calling tools so the executor has a clean API to work with.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/core/mcp.rs` with the module gated behind `#[cfg(feature = "mcp")]`
Add `pub mod mcp;` to `src/core/mod.rs` (feature-gated)
`McpClient` struct that wraps rmcp Client with stdio transport
`McpClient::spawn(command: &str, env: HashMap<String, String>) -> Result<Self>` spawns the MCP server as a child process via `TokioChildProcess`, sends `initialize` request
`McpClient::list_tools(&self) -> Result<Vec<ToolInfo>>` returns available tools with names, descriptions, and input schemas
`McpClient::call_tool(&self, tool: &str, args: serde_json::Value) -> Result<String>` calls a tool and returns the text content of the result
`McpClient::shutdown(self)` terminates the child process gracefully
Each public method uses `tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap().block_on()` (Approach A: block-on per call)
Errors are mapped to `DzError` variants (add `Mcp(String)` variant to error.rs)
Add `Mcp(String)` variant to `DzError` enum in error.rs (feature-gated)
`cargo build --features mcp` succeeds
`cargo test --features mcp` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a NEW file. The rmcp crate provides: rmcp::ServiceExt for initialize, rmcp::model::Tool for tool info, rmcp::transport::TokioChildProcess for stdio. Use tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().block_on() wrapper since the rest of the codebase is synchronous. The runtime is created fresh per call (Approach A from PRD) — subprocess spawn time dominates latency.

userStories.title: Add MCP step execution to executor
userStories.description: As a user, I want MCP steps to execute within the DAG engine with full support for retries, timeouts, template vars, and output capture.
acceptanceCriteria: In executor.rs, add a dispatch branch for `mcp:` steps alongside the existing `cmd:` and `call:` branches
MCP step execution flow: resolve server config (alias lookup in Config or use inline), inject secrets as env vars, spawn McpClient, call_tool, capture result text as stdout
Template variable expansion (`{{var}}`) applied to all mcp.args string values before calling the tool
MCP step results participate in output capture (existing `outputs:` regex patterns work on the text result)
MCP steps respect existing DAG features: `needs:`, `run_if`/`skip_if`, `retry`/`retry_delay`, `timeout`, `for_each`
On MCP error, step status is `Failed` with error message in stderr
Emit `ExecutionEvent::StepStart` and `ExecutionEvent::StepComplete` for TUI display
When mcp feature is disabled and an mcp step is encountered at runtime, return clear error
`cargo test --features mcp` passes
`cargo test` passes (no regression)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Executor is in src/core/executor.rs. Server alias resolution uses Config.mcp_servers HashMap. Secrets injection uses existing SecretsStore from src/core/secrets.rs — call load_secret_env() for each secret name in the server config. Template expansion uses existing expand_template() from src/core/template.rs.

userStories.title: Add Mcp CLI subcommand structure
userStories.description: As a user, I want `workflow mcp` subcommands so I can discover and test MCP servers from the command line.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `Mcp` variant to the CLI subcommand enum in `src/cli/args.rs`
Mcp subcommand has three actions: `ListTools`, `Call`, `Check`
`ListTools` accepts: `<server>` (positional, alias or --server for inline command), `--json` flag
`Call` accepts: `<server>` (positional), `<tool>` (positional), `--arg key=value` (repeatable)
`Check` accepts: `<server>` (positional)
All three actions gated behind `#[cfg(feature = "mcp")]` — without the feature, print helpful error
Add dispatch to `src/cli/mod.rs` for the Mcp subcommand
`cargo build --features mcp` succeeds
`cargo build` succeeds (prints error for mcp subcommand without feature)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CLI args are defined in src/cli/args.rs using clap derive macros. The Mcp subcommand structure mirrors the existing subcommands (run, list, status, etc). Dispatch goes through cli/mod.rs. The actual handlers will be in a new src/cli/mcp.rs file (next stories).

userStories.title: Implement mcp list-tools command
userStories.description: As a workflow author, I want to run `workflow mcp list-tools github` to see all available operations with their parameter schemas so I can write MCP steps without reading external docs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/cli/mcp.rs` with the list-tools handler
Add `pub mod mcp;` to `src/cli/mod.rs` (feature-gated)
Resolve server from config.toml alias or `--server` inline command
Spawn MCP server, initialize, call list_tools via McpClient
Default output: formatted table with tool name, description (truncated), and parameter count
With `--json` flag: output full tool list as JSON including input schemas
Graceful error if server fails to start or initialize
`cargo build --features mcp` succeeds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a NEW file src/cli/mcp.rs. Uses McpClient from core/mcp.rs. Server resolution: if positional arg matches a key in Config.mcp_servers, use that config; otherwise treat as a command string. The --server flag overrides for inline command specification.

userStories.title: Implement mcp call command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run `workflow mcp call github create_issue --arg repo=myorg/myapp --arg title='Bug report'` for ad-hoc tool invocation and testing.
acceptanceCriteria: Add call handler to `src/cli/mcp.rs`
Parse `--arg key=value` pairs into a serde_json::Value object
Resolve server from config.toml alias, inject secrets from secrets store
Spawn McpClient, call_tool with parsed args, print result text to stdout
Exit code 0 on success, non-zero on tool error
Print tool error message (isError flag from MCP result) to stderr
`cargo build --features mcp` succeeds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The --arg key=value pairs need to be parsed into a JSON object. Values should be passed as strings unless they look like numbers or booleans. Secrets injection follows the same pattern as executor.rs — use SecretsStore::load() and load_secret_env().

userStories.title: Implement mcp check command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run `workflow mcp check github` to verify a server starts, initializes, and can list tools — useful for debugging connection issues and credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: Add check handler to `src/cli/mcp.rs`
Resolve server from config.toml alias, inject secrets
Spawn MCP server process, send initialize request, call list_tools
On success: print server name, number of tools available, and list tool names
On failure: print specific error (spawn failure, initialize timeout, credential issue)
Exit code 0 on success, non-zero on failure
`cargo build --features mcp` succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integration tests with echo MCP server
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests using a minimal echo MCP server so CI can validate MCP functionality without external dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `tests/fixtures/echo_mcp_server.sh` — a minimal bash script that implements MCP JSON-RPC over stdio: responds to initialize, tools/list (returns one 'echo' tool), and tools/call (echoes args back as text)
Integration test: executor runs a workflow with an mcp: step using the echo server, captures output
Integration test: `workflow mcp list-tools --server 'bash tests/fixtures/echo_mcp_server.sh'` shows the echo tool
Integration test: `workflow mcp call` with the echo server returns expected output
Integration test: `workflow mcp check` with the echo server reports success
All tests gated behind `#[cfg(feature = "mcp")]`
`cargo test --features mcp` passes including new integration tests
`cargo test` passes (MCP tests skipped without feature)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The echo MCP server script reads JSON-RPC from stdin and writes responses to stdout. It only needs to handle 3 methods: initialize, tools/list, tools/call. Use jq or simple bash string matching for JSON parsing. Integration tests use assert_cmd and tempfile::TempDir like existing tests in tests/cli_tests.rs.

userStories.title: Wire secrets store to MCP credential injection
userStories.description: As a user, I want MCP server credentials (API keys, tokens) resolved from the encrypted secrets store and injected as environment variables to the server process.
acceptanceCriteria: In executor.rs MCP step dispatch: resolve `mcp.secrets` names from SecretsStore via `load_secret_env()`
Secrets are injected as environment variables to the child process (same as cmd: steps)
Secrets from config.toml server definition AND inline YAML secrets are merged (YAML takes precedence)
Secret values are masked in logs via existing `mask_secrets()` function
If a secret name is not found in the secrets store, emit a clear error with the secret name
Unit test: verify secrets are included in the env map passed to McpClient::spawn
Unit test: verify missing secret produces descriptive error
`cargo test --features mcp` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Secrets store is at src/core/secrets.rs with SecretsStore::load(), load_secret_env(), and mask_secrets(). The pattern is: load the store, call load_secret_env(name) for each secret name, add the resulting key=value to the env HashMap that gets passed to the MCP server subprocess.

userStories.title: Render MCP steps in TUI details pane
userStories.description: As a user, I want the TUI details pane to show `mcp: server/tool` with args instead of raw command text when viewing MCP steps.
acceptanceCriteria: In `src/tui/ui.rs`, when rendering step details for an MCP step, show: `mcp: <server>/<tool>` as the command line
If args are present, render them as indented key: value pairs below the mcp line
For alias servers, show the alias name; for inline servers, show the command
MCP steps visually distinguished from cmd: steps (e.g., different label or prefix)
Non-MCP step rendering unchanged
Code compiles both with and without mcp feature (use `#[cfg(feature = "mcp")]` guards)
`cargo build --features mcp` succeeds
`cargo build` succeeds (no regression)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: TUI rendering is in src/tui/ui.rs. The details pane shows step information including the command. For MCP steps, instead of showing a bash command, show the MCP server and tool name in a readable format.

userStories.title: Support template vars in MCP args
userStories.description: As a user, I want to use `{{var}}` template variables and `$SECRET` references in MCP args so I can parameterize tool calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Template expansion (`{{var}}`, `{{date}}`, `{{hostname}}`, `{{step_id.output_name}}`) applied to all string values in mcp.args before calling the tool
Environment variable expansion (`$VAR`) applied to mcp.env values in both config.toml and inline YAML definitions
Nested JSON args: expansion applies recursively to string values in objects and arrays
Non-string values (numbers, booleans, null) are passed through unchanged
Unit test: expand `{{version}}` in mcp.args.tag value
Unit test: expand `$DB_PASSWORD` in mcp.env.DATABASE_URL
Unit test: nested object args with mixed string/number values
`cargo test --features mcp` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Template expansion uses existing expand_template() from src/core/template.rs. The $VAR expansion uses the same pattern as notify/resolve.rs. For JSON args, walk the serde_json::Value tree and apply expand_template() to every Value::String leaf.

userStories.title: Update AI wizard with MCP awareness
userStories.description: As a user, I want the AI wizard to know about my configured MCP servers so it can generate mcp: steps instead of curl commands.
acceptanceCriteria: In `src/core/ai.rs`, when building the AI system prompt, include a section listing configured MCP server aliases and their available tools
System prompt instructs the AI to prefer mcp: steps over curl/shell commands when a matching MCP server is configured
AI prompt includes the mcp: YAML syntax with an example
Only include MCP server info when the mcp feature is enabled and servers are configured
AI-generated workflows with mcp: steps parse correctly through the existing parser
Unit test: verify system prompt includes MCP servers when configured
Unit test: verify system prompt omits MCP section when no servers configured
`cargo test --features mcp` passes
`cargo test` passes (no regression)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: AI integration is in src/core/ai.rs. The system prompt is built in the function that crafts the message to claude/codex/gemini. Add a conditional section that lists MCP server aliases. This does NOT call list_tools (too slow for prompt building) — just lists server names so the AI knows what services are available.

userStories.title: Add MCP section to README
userStories.description: As a user, I want documentation in the README explaining how to configure and use MCP steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Add MCP Integration section to README.md with: overview, config.toml syntax for [mcp.servers], YAML mcp: step syntax with examples
Document CLI commands: `workflow mcp list-tools`, `workflow mcp call`, `workflow mcp check`
Include 2-3 practical examples: GitHub release workflow, database query, file operations
Document the mcp feature flag: `cargo build --features mcp`
Document credential setup via the existing secrets store
Section placed logically within existing README structure
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: README.md is in the repository root. Add the MCP section after the existing features documentation. Keep examples practical and tested.

userStories.title: Create example MCP workflow files
userStories.description: As a user, I want example workflow files that demonstrate MCP step usage so I can learn by example.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `templates/mcp-github-release.yaml` — multi-step workflow: create release, post to Slack, update issue
Create `templates/mcp-db-backup.yaml` — query database, save results, notify on completion
Create `templates/mcp-filesystem-ops.yaml` — read/write files via filesystem MCP server
Each template has a `variables:` section for substitution (consistent with existing template format)
Each template has descriptive comments explaining the MCP steps
Templates parse successfully via `workflow validate`
Templates are registered in `src/core/catalog.rs` via `include_str!()` (consistent with existing bundled templates)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Existing templates are in the templates/ directory and registered in src/core/catalog.rs via include_str!(). Follow the exact same pattern. The variables section uses {{var}} syntax for substitution. Templates should be realistic but not require specific MCP servers to validate.

userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md architecture docs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CLAUDE.md updated with the MCP module documentation so future contributors understand the architecture.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `src/core/mcp.rs` to the key source files table with description: 'MCP client: spawn, initialize, list_tools, call_tool, teardown'
Add `src/cli/mcp.rs` to the key source files table with description: 'CLI subcommands: list-tools, call, check'
Add MCP execution flow to the 'Execution pipeline' section (step between parsing and execution)
Document the mcp: step type alongside cmd: and call: in relevant sections
Document the [mcp.servers] config.toml section in the Config section
Add mcp feature flag to the Build & Test Commands section
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CLAUDE.md is in the repository root. It serves as architecture documentation for Claude Code. Update the existing sections rather than adding a standalone MCP section — integrate MCP references into the execution pipeline, key source files table, and config documentation.

userStories.title: Release preparation for v0.5.0
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the version bumped and changelog updated so the MCP feature can be released.
acceptanceCriteria: Bump version in Cargo.toml from current to 0.5.0
Add CHANGELOG entry for v0.5.0 with: MCP integration summary, new CLI commands, config.toml additions, feature flag documentation
Document that mcp is an opt-in feature: `cargo install workflow --features mcp`
Verify `cargo build --features mcp` produces working binary
Verify `cargo build` produces working binary without MCP (no regression)
Verify `cargo test` and `cargo test --features mcp` both pass
Typecheck passes
